parsed_review.summary: CP00-483 (RP16 governance-core, Risk C, 150 units, RP16.P01.M06.S05-RP16.P02.M02.S22) closes P01 and lays the P02 foundation as a descriptor-only slice. The production_ready claim is supported: (1) deliverable and micro-title distributions reconcile exactly across the prompt baseline, registry REQUIREMENTS, manifest, and contract (150 units tallied: P01=88, P02=62); (2) freezeCp483Result stamps no-runtime/no-write/no-leak flags and validators enforce descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false on every P02 row, including service-sounding micros (persistence boundary, lock acquisition, idempotency, rollback/retry, permission/audit precheck) — no runtime is opened; (3) auth preflight passed (exit_code 0, 1446-byte stdout, valid JSON {"ok":true}); validation-report shows summary_only disallowed (Risk C + security/audit + review-evidence present), source inspected via Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools; (4) authority boundary intact — Claude is read-only and not final approver, enterprise trust claim closed outside this receipt, Hermes used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling and not embedded in product runtime; (5) command-evidence shows all pre-review gates at exit_code 0 and manifest correctly holds production_ready=false / status implemented_pending_review until external approval. One P3 cosmetic finding (handoff open_scope mislabels the next micro as "type and shape rows" when RP16.P02.M03 is "Primary Implementation Slice"); it is mirrored consistently between source and contract with no drift and does not block pack or goal closeout. No P0/P1/P2 issues.

findings.title: Closeout handoff open_scope mislabels next micro as "type and shape rows"
findings.details: The frozen closeout_handoff.open_scope reads: "Continue RP16.P02.M03.S01 onward with the remaining type and shape rows and downstream micros..." However, the Type And Shape Definition work (micro M02) is fully closed by this pack (last_unit_id RP16.P02.M02.S22), and per docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json:1960 the next micro RP16.P02.M03 is titled "Primary Implementation Slice" (its first step RP16.P02.M03.S01 is "Service entrypoint contract"), not additional type/shape rows. The same text is mirrored verbatim in contracts/governance-core-contract.json:5831, so source and contract are internally consistent (no drift) — this is a cosmetic descriptive label, not a structural or boundary defect.
findings.recommendation: Optionally reword the open_scope to reference the Primary Implementation Slice (RP16.P02.M03) instead of "remaining type and shape rows" in a future pack, updating both packages/governance/src/service.js:553 and the contract mirror at contracts/governance-core-contract.json:5831 together to preserve no-drift. Non-blocking for CP00-483 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic nit; descriptor-only boundary, counts, attestations, and authority boundary all verified. Does not block pack or goal closeout. Recommend deferring the wording fix to a downstream pack.
